title: LATAM Maternity & Newborn Care Platform
problem: Maternity care in LATAM is fragmented. Prenatal care often inadequate, especially in low-income populations. High rates of maternal mortality (Brazil: 60/100K vs US: 17/100K). No continuous monitoring during pregnancy. Postpartum care minimal. No education on warning signs. C-section rates very high (55% in Brazil vs WHO recommendation 15%).
solution: Maternity care platform. Prenatal appointment tracking and reminders. Symptom monitoring (AI triage for warning signs). Educational content (video, articles). Postpartum check-ins. Newborn development tracking. Connection to community health workers. Integration with OB-GYN EMRs.
technology: Mobile app, AI symptom triage, appointment scheduling, content delivery, community health worker dashboard, EMR integration
target_customer: Health insurers (reduce complications), public health systems, OB-GYN practices, employers (maternity benefits)
ai_type: Symptom triage for warning signs (preeclampsia, etc.)
traction_signals: High maternal mortality rates driving intervention
regulatory_notes: Medical advice regulations, pregnancy data privacy, integration with public health programs
varden_fit: MEDIUM FIT - Consumer-facing but critical public health need. Could partner with Ministry of Health for scale. High impact, potential government contracts.
notes: Maternal health is complex but high-impact. Brazil's maternal mortality is 3x US rate. Public health contracts possible. Strong mission-driven angle.
